message:
test(e2e): harden mission_tx_ledger harness with shared rch guards library

Consolidate rch readiness and timeout logic by adopting lib_rch_guards.sh,
replacing bespoke probe/smoke/timeout helpers with shared library functions.

Changes:
- Source lib_rch_guards.sh and call rch_init for standardized semantics
- Use ensure_rch_ready instead of manual rch workers probe
- Add separate RCH_COMPILE_TIMEOUT_SECS for build phase timeouts
- Refactor run_rch_timed to enforce timeout availability fail-closed
- Extract scenario 2 test filters to array variable for clarity
- Add preflight_timeout_artifact_label() for better timeout labeling
- Remove ~100 lines of duplicate probe/timeout helper functions
- Update beads issue tracking metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: replaced the optional FrankenRedis sibling path deps (`../../../frankenredis/...`) with pinned git deps against `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenredis` at `rev 7b00450d924e8048073745736356ae0e45a592af`.
- `Cargo.lock`: path packages for `fr-config`, `fr-expire`, `fr-persist`, `fr-protocol`, and `fr-store` now resolve to that git source.
- `.cargo/config.toml.example`: added a local override stanza so developers with a sibling checkout can still patch the same crates back to local paths intentionally.

Why:
- WhiteFox's prior worker failure (`failed to read .../frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`) was caused by manifest-level path assumptions inside this repo rather than by the worker's Cargo/test invocation itself.
- After the manifest change, two different `rch` workers resolved FrankenRedis through git instead of requiring an out-of-repo sibling checkout.

Validation (all offloaded through `rch` where compilation/test work was involved):
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-2-test cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib command_uses_rch -- --nocapture` -> remote success on `vmi1152480`; test `build_coord::tests::command_uses_rch_detects_common_prefix_shapes` passed.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-2-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features redis-session --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` with exit 0 after fetching and compiling the git-pinned FrankenRedis crates.
- `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml .cargo/config.toml.example Cargo.lock` -> clean.

Residual note:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` is classified by `rch` as non-compilation and did not provide a meaningful remote validation artifact, so I did not count it as evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13Z"}]}

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: replaced the optional Frankentui subcrate sibling path deps (`../../../frankentui/...`) with pinned git deps against `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui` at `rev 18facd3f2a2c51b8b06ff589e6b905e6a63f5a96`.
- `.cargo/config.toml.example`: extended the existing Frankentui patch block so local sibling-checkout development can still intentionally override `ftui`, `ftui-core`, `ftui-render`, `ftui-layout`, `ftui-style`, `ftui-widgets`, `ftui-runtime`, and `ftui-backend`.

Why:
- ProudMeadow's topology preflight artifact showed the current first missing remote manifest was `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankentui/crates/ftui-backend/Cargo.toml`, indicating a second ambient sibling-layout assumption after the FrankenRedis fix.

Validation:
- `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml .cargo/config.toml.example Cargo.lock` -> clean.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-3-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features tui-dashboard --message-format short` -> remote execution on `vmi1152480` reached and resolved the Frankentui git crates successfully (`ftui-core`, `ftui-render`, `ftui-layout`, `ftui-style`, `ftui-widgets`, `ftui-runtime`, `ftui-backend` all appeared as git-sourced in the remote check output).
- That remote check then failed with unrelated pre-existing `policy_diagnostics.rs` compile errors (`KillSwitch.level`, missing telemetry fields, missing `snapshot` methods) rather than any missing Frankentui path-manifest error.

Residual note:
- I did not modify `Cargo.lock` for this slice because the workspace was already carrying the same pinned Frankentui git revision; the lockfile still contains duplicate `ftui-*` entries, but the active manifest path used by the remote worker resolved through the git source rather than a sibling checkout.
- Treat the remaining `tui-dashboard` validation failure as blocked on the existing `policy_diagnostics.rs` compile break, not on remote worker topology for Frankentui.","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:14:04Z"}]}

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs`: added `command_uses_rch`, `is_heavy_cargo_command`, `requires_rch_offload`, and `recommended_rch_prefix()`; the parser now handles env-prefixed commands and `rch exec -- cargo ...` wrappers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`: added `policy.rch.heavy_compute` so non-human `SendText` requests invoking heavy cargo without `rch` now return `RequireApproval` with an `rch` prefix recommendation.
- Added focused unit tests covering wrapped/unwrapped heavy cargo detection and policy decisions.

Validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` -> remote success on `vmi1149989`.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` in 2m07s (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Initial remote compile exposed an implementation bug (`shell_words::split` unresolved in `build_coord.rs`); fixed by switching to existing `shlex::split`, then reran the remote check successfully.

Remaining blocker:
- Targeted remote `cargo test` commands for the new unit tests are currently blocked by worker topology drift: scheduler repeatedly sends them to `vmi1227854`, which exits 101 because `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml` is missing on that worker. Implementation is complete; remote test execution is infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.2","title":"Unified agent detection module merging runtime + filesystem probes","description":"## Context
FrankenTerm currently detects agents at runtime via `agent_correlator.rs` (3-layer: pattern engine → pane title → process name). The `franken-agent-detection` crate adds a 4th layer: filesystem-based installation probing that detects agents whether or not they're running.

**Related prior work:**
- `ft-dug` (CLOSED): Smart Environment Detection epic — included basic agent presence detection as part of broader environment probing. This bead extends that with the dedicated `franken-agent-detection` crate for much deeper, more structured agent inventory.
- `ft-2l27x.10` (CLOSED): Agent session correlation & metadata capture — established `AgentMetadata` struct and agent type detection via pattern engine + pane title + process name. This bead builds on that by adding filesystem-based detection as a 4th layer.
- `ft-dug.4` (within ft-dug): Environment detection API — detected agents as one of many environment signals. This bead makes agent detection first-class with a dedicated module and richer data.

## Scope
Create `agent_detection.rs` module that unifies both approaches:

1. **Startup detection** (new, via franken-agent-detection):
   - Call `detect_installed_agents()` at FrankenTerm startup
   - Cache the `InstalledAgentDetectionReport` in a global `OnceLock`
   - Report includes: slug, detected bool, evidence strings, root_paths for each of 9 agents
   - Supported agents: claude, codex, gemini, cursor, cline, opencode, aider, windsurf, continue
   - Detection probes: config directories (~/.claude/, ~/.codex/), binary presence in PATH, version files

2. **Runtime correlation** (existing, enhanced):
   - When `agent_correlator` identifies an agent in a pane, cross-reference with installation data
   - Enrich `PaneAgentState` with: installed version, config_path, binary_path from detection report
   - If agent_correlator detects an agent NOT in the installation report, log a diagnostic warning ("agent running but not found on filesystem — possible containerized or remote agent")
   - If detection report shows an agent installed but correlator never sees it running, note as "installed but idle"

3. **Unified `AgentInventory` struct**:
   - `installed: Vec<InstalledAgentDetectionEntry>` — what's on the system
   - `running: HashMap<PaneId, PaneAgentState>` — what's active in which pane
   - `summary()` method returning counts and categorized agent list (installed-only, running, installed+running)
   - `agent_by_slug(slug: &str)` for quick lookup
   - Serde-serializable for IPC and robot mode output

4. **Event bus integration**:
   - Emit `AgentDetected { pane_id, agent_type, detection_method }` when correlator identifies agent
   - Emit `AgentExited { pane_id, agent_type, exit_code }` when agent process exits
   - Events carry detection method (filesystem, pane_title, process_name, pattern_engine) for diagnostics

## Testing Requirements
- `test_startup_detection_all_9`: fixture dirs for all 9 agents → all detected
- `test_startup_detection_none`: empty fixtures → empty report, no panics

- `test_unknown_agent_diagnostic`: correlator finds agent not in report → warning logged
- `test_installed_but_idle`: agent in report but never correlated → correct category
- `test_inventory_summary_counts`: verify installed/running/both counts
- `test_event_bus_detected`: agent starts → AgentDetected event with correct fields
- `test_event_bus_exited`: agent exits → AgentExited event with exit code
- `test_feature_flag_off`: cfg(not(feature = "agent-detection")) → correlator works standalone
- All tests use structured JSON logging with agent_slug, detection_method, timing

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 9 agent types detected at startup if their configs/binaries exist
- agent_correlator runtime detection enriched with installation metadata
- Unified inventory queryable via single method call
- Detection runs in < 50ms (pure filesystem probes)
- Graceful fallback if franken-agent-detection feature disabled
- Event bus emits agent lifecycle events with detection method provenance
- Prior work from ft-dug and ft-2l27x.10 preserved and extended (not duplicated)

 {"id":"ft-dr6zv.2.4","title":"Auto-generate agent configuration files for detected agents","description":"## Context
Once FrankenTerm knows which agents are installed, it can auto-generate configuration files to teach each agent about FrankenTerm's capabilities — creating a seamless integration loop where detected agents automatically learn how to use FrankenTerm's robot mode, MCP server, and pane management.

## Scope
1. For each detected agent, generate agent-specific config snippets:
   - **Claude Code**: generate/update SKILL.md with FrankenTerm robot mode commands, MCP tool descriptions
   - **Codex**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections with FrankenTerm integration patterns
   - **Gemini**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **Cursor**: generate .cursorrules with FrankenTerm integration patterns and robot mode reference
   - **Cline**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **OpenCode**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **Aider**: generate CONVENTIONS.md section with FrankenTerm workflow
   - **Windsurf**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections
   - **Continue**: generate/update AGENTS.md sections

2. Auto-configuration trigger:
   - On first startup after agent detected, offer to generate configs (interactive prompt)
   - Robot mode: `ft robot agents configure --agent claude --dry-run` (non-interactive)
   - `ft robot agents configure --all` to generate for all detected agents
   - Never overwrite existing configs — merge or append only
   - Backup existing file before any modification (`.bak` with timestamp)

3. Config templates (per agent type):
   - Include FrankenTerm robot mode command reference (all `ft robot` subcommands)
   - Include MCP server connection details (if ft MCP feature enabled)
   - Include pane management and search commands
   - Include agent detection self-reference (agent can query its own detection status)
   - Templates stored in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/agent_config_templates/`

4. Config placement rules:
   - Project-level configs: placed in current working directory (SKILL.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules)
   - Global configs: placed in agent's global config directory (~/.claude/, ~/.codex/, etc.)
   - Configurable via `--scope project|global` flag

## Testing Requirements
- `test_config_claude_skill_md`: generates valid SKILL.md with ft robot commands
- `test_config_codex_agents_md`: generates valid AGENTS.md section for Codex
- `test_config_cursor_rules`: generates valid .cursorrules with FrankenTerm patterns

- `test_config_merge_idempotent`: run twice → no duplicate content added
- `test_config_backup_created`: before modification, `.bak` file created with original content
- `test_config_dry_run`: `--dry-run` outputs what would be generated, writes no files
- `test_config_dry_run_json`: `--dry-run --format json` returns structured preview
- `test_config_syntax_valid`: generated configs parse without error in each agent's format
- `test_config_project_scope`: `--scope project` places files in working directory
- `test_config_global_scope`: `--scope global` places files in agent's config directory
- `test_config_template_completeness`: each template includes robot mode ref, MCP details, pane commands
- `test_config_mcp_conditional`: MCP section only included when ft MCP feature is enabled
- All tests use structured JSON logging with agent_slug, config_type, scope, file_path

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config generation works for all 9 agent types
- Dry-run mode shows what would be generated without writing
- Existing agent configs are never overwritten (append/merge only)
- Backup created before any modification
- Generated configs are syntactically valid for each agent's format
- Robot mode command available for programmatic config management
- Merge is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- All tests pass with structured logging

## Test and Observability Plan

### 1. Unit Test Expansion (per-module, run in CI)

- `test_runtime_correlation_enrichment`: when agent_correlator detects "claude" in pane, cross-reference adds version/config_path from detection report
- `test_runtime_unknown_agent_diagnostic`: agent_correlator detects agent NOT in installation report → diagnostic log emitted
- `test_agent_inventory_installed_field`: AgentInventory.installed contains exactly the detected agents
- `test_agent_inventory_running_field`: AgentInventory.running maps pane_id → PaneAgentState with enrichment
- `test_agent_inventory_summary`: summary() returns correct counts for installed, running, configured
- `test_feature_flag_graceful_fallback`: when agent-detection feature disabled, existing correlator still works (no compile errors, no panics)

Upstream `franken-agent-detection` sorts `root_paths` alphabetically after probing, so using the first matching root (or `root_paths.first()` in `main`) is not equivalent to provider probe precedence. This could select the wrong global config file/root when multiple detected roots exist, especially for Cursor and other providers whose preferred dotdir root sorts after `.config`. Fixed by adding provider-specific root precedence in `agent_correlator` and reusing that helper from `main.rs` for global target selection. Added focused regressions for both config-path inference and config creation targeting.  {"id":"ft-dug","title":"[EPIC] Smart Environment Detection: auto-configure based on detected setup","description":"# [EPIC] Smart Environment Detection

## Mission
Automatically detect the user's environment and configure wa optimally, reducing setup friction.

## Why This Matters
Users struggle with configuration:
- "Which settings do I need?"
- "Why isn't it detecting my agents?"
- "What's the right poll interval?"

Smart detection provides:
- **Zero-config start**: Works out of the box
- **Optimal defaults**: Tuned for detected environment
- **Guided setup**: Suggests improvements

## Scope

### Environment Detection
- WezTerm version and capabilities
- Shell type (bash, zsh, fish)
- OSC 133 support
- Agent presence and types
- Remote panes (SSH)
- System resources (CPU, RAM)

### Auto-configuration
- Poll intervals based on system load
- Pattern packs based on detected agents
- Storage paths based on OS conventions
- Safety defaults based on environment

### Detection API
```rust
pub struct DetectedEnvironment {
    wezterm: WeztermInfo,
    shell: ShellInfo,
    osc_133_support: bool,
    agents: Vec<DetectedAgent>,
    remotes: Vec<RemoteHost>,
    system: SystemInfo,
}
```

### CLI Integration
- `wa doctor` shows detection results
- `wa setup` uses detection for suggestions
- Config shows effective settings with sources

## Success Criteria
- New users need no configuration to start
- Detection is accurate for common setups
- Suggestions improve user experience

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Environment detection API complete
- [ ] Auto-configuration uses detection
- [ ] wa doctor shows detection results
- [ ] Suggestions are actionable
- [ ] Tests cover detection scenarios

## Testing
- Unit tests for probes and recommendation rules.
- Integration tests for doctor/setup output.
- E2E artifacts include detection results and rationale.
